fields.description:
Determine empirically whether the plan-review novelty scorer works: post-flip field data shows remediation-eligible reviews produced ZERO novelty drops, consistent either with a healthy precision-first quadrant or with a silently failing scorer (the fail-safe returns zero drops on error). CONSTRAINT DISCOVERED IN PLANNING: the live eval runner CANNOT run this prompt - `run_case` in src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py:156 dispatches only criterion ids, code-review prompt ids, and three hardcoded agentic reviewers, so `run_eval("plan-review-novelty")` raises ValueError per case (a separate bug ticket records that gap, linked discovered_from this task). This task therefore probes the RUNTIME scorer path directly - `score_novelty` (src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py:759), as invoked by `_score_floor_novelty` in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py - using the repo venv with the `[agents]` extra (pydantic_ai) + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, against REAL inputs reconstructed from persisted sidecars. Context: the rising floor is already unconditional (operator decision 2026-07-11, in lieu of this eval), so the outcome no longer gates activation - a defective-scorer outcome drives a repair bug, a healthy outcome closes the loop with evidence.

Method: a local, uncommitted probe script (workpad; not landed - this is an analysis task, its durable output is the ticket comments). The eligible-review set is defined as: plan_review sidecars (REVIEW_RESULT events under .tickets-tracker/<ticket>/) whose `coverage.remediation.eligible` is true, dated after 2026-07-12. For each, reconstruct (advisory findings of round N, surfaced findings of round N-1) and call score_novelty live. "Sidecar example" = a REVIEW_RESULT event cited by ticket id + event timestamp.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The probe runs score_novelty live over the reconstructed input pairs from ALL post-2026-07-12 remediation-eligible plan-review sidecars (or explicitly reports the count found if zero/fewer than expected). [operator-attested] A ticket comment records the probe invocation, the number of eligible sidecars probed, and the resulting novelty-score distribution (min/median/max, and the count of findings falling in the drop quadrant novelty >= 0.7 AND priority < 0.4).
- [ ] The healthy/defective conclusion applies this decision rule, stated in the conclusion comment: DEFECTIVE if (a) the live call errors or returns an empty/unanswerable map for > 20% of probed pairs (silent fail-safe), or (b) known-carryover findings (same norm_id as a prior-round finding) fail to score novelty < 0.7 in the majority of cases; HEALTHY if carryovers score low, genuinely new findings score high, and the observed zero drops are fully explained by priorities >= 0.4. [operator-attested] The conclusion comment states which branch held, citing at least two sidecar examples (ticket id + event timestamp each).
- [ ] The non-live-runnable eval gap is recorded: a bug ticket exists for the missing plan-review-novelty dispatch arm in eval_solver.run_case, linked discovered_from this task. Oracle: `rebar deps 0730-6ceb-88c3-430c` shows the discovered_from link.
- [ ] If the scorer is DEFECTIVE, a scorer-repair bug ticket is created and linked discovered_from this task; if HEALTHY, the conclusion comment says so explicitly and no repair bug is filed.
